req.title: Create medication request
req.statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-008 governs MedicationRequest creation by prescribers. Three distinct risk areas drive test selection: (1) happy-path non-controlled prescription succeeds (EP – valid prescriber partition), (2) controlled-substance CII request without schedule verification is rejected 403 (NEG/DT – error pathway), (3) boundary between CII and CIII schedule codes determines whether verification gate fires (BVA – schedule boundary). A fourth STC test covers the full lifecycle state transition from draft to active to pharmacy-notified. Each test uses a pre-seeded auth token fixture and a disposable request ID generator to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_prescriber_creates_non_controlled_medication_request
preconditions: Caller holds a valid JWT with role=prescriber
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with a well-formed FHIR R4 payload for a non-controlled drug
expected: Pharmacy notification endpoint received the request ID
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_prescriber_creates_non_controlled_medication_request(
    prescriber_auth_headers,   # <fixture>
    fhir_client,               # <fixture> wraps requests.Session
    pharmacy_spy,              # <fixture> captures outbound notifications
):
    rid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "id": rid,
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "308460", "display": "Amoxicillin 500mg"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "extension": [{"url": "schedule", "valueString": "none"}]
    }
    resp = fhir_client.post(
        "/MedicationRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "MedicationRequest"
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert pharmacy_spy.was_notified(body["id"])

tests.name: test_cii_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403
preconditions: Caller holds a valid JWT with role=prescriber
No schedule-verification token is present in the request
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with schedule=CII and no x-schedule-verification header
expected: No MedicationRequest resource created in the store
trace: R-HLT-008: 403 without schedule verification for CII
R-HLT-008: controlled substances require schedule verification
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_cii_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403(
    prescriber_auth_headers,   # <fixture>
    fhir_client,               # <fixture>
    pharmacy_spy,              # <fixture>
    fhir_store,                # <fixture> direct store query helper
):
    rid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "id": rid,
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049502", "display": "Oxycodone 10mg"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "extension": [{"url": "schedule", "valueString": "CII"}]
    }
    # Intentionally omit x-schedule-verification header
    resp = fhir_client.post(
        "/MedicationRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "SCHEDULE_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED"
    assert not fhir_store.exists("MedicationRequest", rid)
    assert not pharmacy_spy.was_notified(rid)

tests.name: test_cii_boundary_schedule_verification_gate_fires_at_exactly_cii
preconditions: Caller holds a valid JWT with role=prescriber
Two requests prepared: one at CII (boundary requiring verification), one at CIII (boundary not requiring verification)
No x-schedule-verification header is attached to either request
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with schedule=CIII (just below CII gate) without verification header – expect 201
POST /MedicationRequest with schedule=CII (at gate boundary) without verification header – expect 403
Assert gate fires precisely at CII and not at CIII
expected: CIII request returns HTTP 201 (no verification required)
Gate is exclusive to CII and above (boundary is inclusive)
trace: R-HLT-008: controlled substances schedule verification boundary
R-HLT-008: 403 without schedule verification for CII
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

@pytest.mark.parametrize("schedule,expected_status", [
    ("CIII", 201),   # just below CII gate – no verification needed
    ("CII",  403),   # at gate boundary – verification required
])
def test_cii_boundary_schedule_verification_gate_fires_at_exactly_cii(
    schedule,
    expected_status,
    prescriber_auth_headers,   # <fixture>
    fhir_client,               # <fixture>
):
    rid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "id": rid,
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "TEST-CTRL", "display": "Test Controlled"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "extension": [{"url": "schedule", "valueString": schedule}]
    }
    # No x-schedule-verification header for either case
    resp = fhir_client.post(
        "/MedicationRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=prescriber_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"schedule={schedule}: expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_medication_request_state_transitions_draft_to_active_to_notified
preconditions: Caller holds a valid JWT with role=prescriber
fhir-lite service is reachable and pharmacy stub is active
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with status=draft – assert stored resource status==active (draft -> active transition)
Poll pharmacy_spy to assert notification event fired (active -> notified transition)
expected: Immediate POST response status == active (system auto-activates on persist)
Subsequent GET confirms persisted status == active
Pharmacy notification received within allowed window (state: notified)
trace: R-HLT-008: state transition draft -> active -> pharmacy-notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
import time

def test_medication_request_state_transitions_draft_to_active_to_notified(
    prescriber_auth_headers,   # <fixture>
    fhir_client,               # <fixture>
    pharmacy_spy,              # <fixture>
):
    rid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "id": rid,
        "status": "draft",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "308460", "display": "Amoxicillin 500mg"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "extension": [{"url": "schedule", "valueString": "none"}]
    }
    post_resp = fhir_client.post(
        "/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=prescriber_auth_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    assert post_resp.json()["status"] == "active"   # draft -> active

    get_resp = fhir_client.get(
        f"/MedicationRequest/{rid}", headers=prescriber_auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"    # persisted state confirmed

    deadline = time.time() + 5
    while time.time() < deadline:
        if pharmacy_spy.was_notified(rid):
            break
        time.sleep(0.2)
    assert pharmacy_spy.was_notified(rid), "pharmacy not notified within 5 s"  # active -> notified
